zh.Northleigh, Devon, England, United Kingd: 英國英格蘭戴文諾瑟利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hull於1628年出生在英國戴文諾瑟利時，她的父親Rev. Joseph Hull32歲，母親First wife of Rev. Joseph Hull29歲。她於1642年與Captain John Heard在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼越可爾吉特利結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她於1635年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加。她去世於1706年11月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾斯特拉福德多福 姆姆享年78歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾斯特拉福德多福。

nameMeanings.name: Hull
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Hulle, a pet form of Hugh or of its common diminutives Hulin, Hulot (see Hewlett and Huling ).

English: in southwest England and the west and central Midlands sometimes a topographical or habitational name for someone who lived on or by a hill (Middle English atte hulle, from Old English hyll), or from a place with this name. However, this word and the derived names will have usually assumed the standard form Hill in modern times, as in the case of Hill (Gloucestershire), which was usually spelt Hull or Hulle during the Middle Ages. Hull with this origin was also once the name of two other places, now lost, one in Great Budworth (Cheshire), and the other in Inkpen (Berkshire). See also Hell .

English: perhaps a habitational name from Kingston upon Hull in East Yorkshire, which takes its name from the river Hull (perhaps related to Danish hul ‘hole, hollow’, or perhaps a British name based on the root seul- ‘mud’).

variants.name: Huller
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, from Middle English huller (see Hull ). This type of topographic name formation with -er, equivalent to atte Hull, is common in southern England and absent from northern England. See Hiller and Heller .
English: in Yorkshire, a late variant of   Hulley  .
English: variant of Hillier from Middle English huller ‘roofer’, found in Western and southern England as far east as Sussex.

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hull was born in 1628, in Northleigh, Devon, England, her father, Rev. Joseph Hull, was 32 and her mother, First wife of Rev. Joseph Hull, was 29. She married Captain John Heard in 1642, in Kittery, Yorkshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 7 daughters. She immigrated to British Colonial America in 1635. She died on 30 November 1706, in Dover MM, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 78, and was buried in Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

Joseph Hull(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1642년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크실르 키터이에서 Captain John Heard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1635년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아로 이민을 떠났다. 그녀는 1706년 11월 30일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 도버에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 도버에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hull nasceu em 1628, em Northleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Rev. Joseph Hull, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, First wife of Rev. Joseph Hull, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Captain John Heard em 1642, em Kittery, Yorkshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela imigrou para o British Colonial America em 1635. Ela faleceu em 30 de novembro de 1706, em Dover MM, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, com 78 anos, e foi sepultada em Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hull родилась в 1628 г. в местности нортлиг, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Rev. Joseph Hull было 32 года, а матери, которую звали First wife of Rev. Joseph Hull, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Captain John Heard в 1642 г. в местности киттири, еркшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она иммигрировала в бритиш колоняль амирика в 1635 году. Она умерла 30 ноября 1706 г. в местности довир мм, страффорд, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 78 лет и была похоронена в местности довир, страффорд, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Hull nació en 1628, en Northleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Rev. Joseph Hull, tenía 32 años y su mamá, First wife of Rev. Joseph Hull, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Captain John Heard en 1642, en Kittery, Yorkshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 7 hijas. Inmigró a British Colonial America en 1635. Murió el 30 de noviembre de 1706, en Dover MM, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultada en Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.
